Meta Data v8

Event Name/Title

Krishna and Sudama

Event Date

01 Oct 1989

Event Location

India, Mumbai

Event Type

Other

Transcriber

Amrita Radhe Devi Dasi

Completed

15 Apr 2021

Source Audio

 http://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1989/89-061-Story%20Of%20Sri%20Sudama%20Vishnu%20PUran%201_19_65%20(Mumbai)-OT-dt_89_10_01.mp3

Source Video

NA

Verse

VP 1.19.65

Tags – Places

Dwaraka, Vaikuntha

Tags – Pastimes

Sudama Vipra visits Dwaraka, Srila Rupa Goswami cooks a feast on Srila Sanatana Goswami’s appearance day

Tags – Persons

Lord Krishna, Rukmini Devi, Sudama, Srila Rupa Goswami, Srila Sanatana Goswami, Haridas Thakur

Tags – Themes

Transcendental Sound, where is Vaikuntha, Power of Bhakti

Lecture Transcription

namo mahā-vadānyāya
kṛṣṇa-prema-pradāya te
kṛṣṇāya kṛṣṇa-caitanya-
nāmne gaura-tviṣe namaḥ

[CC Madhya 19.53]

[“O most munificent incarnation! You are Kṛṣṇa Himself appearing as Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya Mahāprabhu. You have assumed the golden color of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī, and You are widely distributing pure love of Kṛṣṇa. We offer our respectful obeisances unto You.”]

namo brahmanya-devāya
go-brāhmaṇa-hitāya ca
jagad-hitāya kṛṣṇāya
govindāya namo namaḥ

[Vishnu Puran 1.19.65, CC Madhya 13.77]

Translation

 [‘Let me offer my respectful obeisances unto Lord Kṛṣṇa, who is the worshipable Deity for all brahminical men, who is the well-wisher of cows and brāhmaṇas, and who is always benefiting the whole world. I offer my repeated obeisances to the Personality of Godhead, known as Kṛṣṇa and Govinda.’]

Supreme Lord Krishna is affectionate to Brahmins and Cows

[Maharaj repeats the above sloka two times] This shloka is describing the glory of the Supreme Lord Sri Krishna. Although the lord is equal to all, he is the friend and father of all living beings, he is especially affectionate toward the brahmins and the cows. Why is this? because the goal of life is for all living beings to be freed from the sufferings to the conditional consciousness based in ignorance and illusion. The real platform of happiness is in knowledge of our eternal relationship with the supreme lord. It is said in Bhagvad Gita,

mahātmānas tu māṁ pārtha
daivīṁ prakṛtim āśritāḥ
bhajanty ananya-manaso
jṣātvā bhūtādim avyayam

[BG 9.13]

[O son of Pṛthā, those who are not deluded, the great souls, are under the protection of the divine nature. They are fully engaged in devotional service because they know Me as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, original and inexhaustible.]

The Mahatmas, the great souls they are under the protection of the divine energy of the lord. Therefore, in great happiness they are constantly engaged in serving the lord with love. Essentially it is the nature of every living being - To know God and love God. But due to ignorance and due to conditioning of material enjoyment we have forgotten the essence of our real identity. Therefore, the Lord who is all merciful, is always calling the conditioned souls suffering in this material world back to the real consciousness within themselves. And although as the Paramatma, the lord who is seated within the heart of everyone is giving us many indications, because we are constantly experiencing life through the external forms via our senses therefore we cannot hear or perceive the disa… divine presence or message of the lord within our heart. Thus, the lord speaks through the brahmins, so that we can hear his voice through our senses and traverse the world to real peace, real prosperity for the benefit of all.

And the cows they provide, like our own mother, the most important and nourishing food. And this food provided by the cows in form of milk nourishes our brain with the finer tissues by which we can grasp the subtle conceptions of spiritual understanding, therefore it is said in the Vedas that you can judge a society, how spiritual or material it may be by the simple method of seeing how the cows are being treated. To the degrees the cows are being treated with compassion and mercy, they are happy and feeling protected to that degree you will find spirituality, piety and religion in the society. And to the degree we see cows in anxiety, being exploited and being murdered to that degree we will find sinfulness, lack of spiritual awareness and gross materialism.

So, in the Srimad Bhagvatam Maharaj Parikshit who is inquiring from Sukadev Goswami about the real goals of life and Sukadev Goswami was explaining to him the pastimes of Lord Sri Krishna in the tenth canto. How lord Sri Krishna personally came to this world in the form of a cowherd boy to show the importance of giving respect to the brahmins, those spiritual personalities who have surrendered their lives to the Lord and giving protection to the cows.

Transcendental Sound Vibration

Haa… Maharaj Parikshit was finding great satisfaction in hearing these narrations, in fact he approached Sukadeva Goswami with words of glorification that, “The more I hear the pastimes of the lord and his devotees the more I am anxious to hear. Dear Sukdev, this message that you are narrating in the form of Srimad Bhagvatam in completely the opposite of material sound vibration.” When we hear the material sound vibration, we enjoy it for some time but after hearing it again and again and again it becomes very tiresome but spiritual sound vibrations is ever increasing in the taste of sweetness that one can derive from it. Just like there was a very famous poem - A Rose is a Rose is a Rose. And this became very famous because it sounds very sweet because the rose is very sweet therefore when we think of this ah... we think of the rose and it is very sweet. A rose is a rose is a rose.

Now you say it once, twice, three times, five times, a hundred times, a thousand times and you never want to hear this poem again. You become very tired of it. You don't even want to see a rose again. A rose is a rose is a rose is a rose is a rose is a rose...a. pheww... How many times can you say it, even the greatest literary appreciators they can only say it a few times before they want to talk about something else.

But devotees of the Lord, many great saints in the sannyasa ashram as well as in the grihastha ashram they chant the holy names, hare krishna, hare krishna, krishna krishna, hare hare. hare rama hare rama rama rama hare hare. Two - three hours a day. I know some people that chanted twelve to fourteen hours a day, I know some man in Vrindavan who have been chanting every day for the past seventy years for twelve to fourteen hours a day. hare krishna, hare krishna, krishna krishna, hare hare. hare rama hare rama rama rama hare hare And are they tired of it? Do they want to hear something else? They want to hear it more and more and more. The more they chant it, the more their thirst to taste more of the mantra awakens in their heart. Because you see, in the transcendental sound vibration the sound vibration is non-different than the object which it is describing. nāma cintāmaṇiḥ kṛṣṇaś caitanya-rasa-vigrahaḥ [CC Madhya 17.133]. This means that in the name of Krishna, all the opulence, all the sweetness, all the glory, all the divine presence of God is within his name in full. By Chanting the name Krishna, by chanting, the name Rama you are directly in contact with the Supreme source of everything. Gradually through this process, our heart becomes purified and we can experience God directly in his holy name.

Naamacharya - Srila Haridas Thakura

Haridas Thakura, one of the great associates of Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. He would chant the names of Krishna three lacs every day, before eating or sleeping. And when he was in his old age, an invalid, he still continued his vow of chanting. And the Muslims, who at that time were in the rule of India, the emperor did not want this Sanatana Dharma faith, this Hindu faith to spread. Haridas Thakura was born in a Muslim family, so they arrested him, “Why are you chanting these Hindu names of God? Why don't you give it up?” He explained to them, “What is this Hindu and Muslim, this is all nonsense, God is one. Sanatana Dharma is the essence of all religion, Sanatana Dharma means to know God, serve God and surrender to God, Islam means to submissively surrender to God, it does not mean to be a Muslim. Sanatana Dharma means to surrender to supreme Lord. Krishna says,

sarva-dharmān parityajya
mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja
ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo
mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ

[Bg 18.66]

[Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.]

Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto me. Religion means to love God and surrender unto God. And the most glorious method of surrender to God is to cry out his holy name and glorify his name, this is not a sectarian process.” So off-course what happened is that the hindu priests who were at that time very corrupt, they were called the smarta brahmins, they were simply professionals, they were performing rituals, performing puja, giving diksha as a profession. They had no realization, or love or compassion toward the fallen soul of the age. They simply wanted to exploit them for money and take advantage of their high birth. So, they did not like Haridas Thakura because Haridas Thakura was teaching that anyone who chants the name of God and develops love for God is higher than the brahmins.

So, the Brahmins were thinking ‘My God! if he teaches this message and everyone chants, we will be out of business, we will go bankrupt.’ And you know how businessmen they are willing to do anything and everything to make their business strong. So, they were thinking that ‘we have to somehow or the other devise a plan to rid ourselves of this competitor.’ At the same time the high priests at that time of the Muslim religion they didn't like him because many Muslims were converting to become his followers, chanting the names of Krishna. So all priests together came to the haa… to the zamindar and to the governor and they complained, “This man must be arrested and killed.” So, Haridas Thakura came into the assembly of these very, very powerful government officials and when they saw his beauty, they saw his humility, they saw the compassion of his heart. Their hearts melted. They said, “Haridas, personally, we are the kings, we are the governors, we actually want to be your disciples too, but we cannot because what would the people say. These people are complaining so much about you, we ask you just one thing… just if you have to chant, chant private somewhere but not so loud. Stop telling others about this and we will let you go but if you continue this way, we will have to kill you.” Haridas Thakur, he said that, “To end my life, that is no problem I have to die anyway. But to give up chanting the name of God, that is million, billion, trillion times worse than death.” He said, “You take my body cut it into a million pieces and take each piece and cut it into a million pieces , take each of those pieces and cut it into a million pieces and find each one of those tiny little specs and cut it into a million pieces and you will find that each one of those little insignificant atomic specs will be chanting hare krishna, hare krishna, krishna krishna, hare hare. hare rama hare rama rama rama hare hare.”

Absolute Nature of the Transcendental Sound

Because the transcendental sound of Krishna's glorification is eternally blissful to the heart. So, Maharaj Parikshit was describing that the pastimes of the lord are of the same nature as the name. Factually when the Lord’s glories are being chanted, the lord is enacting his pastimes through transcendental sound vibrations right here and now. It is not that the lord simply performed the lifting of Govardhan hill five thousand years ago but because he is absolute, wherever devotees repeat the narration of the Lord lifting the Govardhan hill, at that time that pastimes is being enacted if we hear it with faith.

So, Maharaj Parikshit was finding great delight in hearing the message of Sukadev Goswami, “Therefore O Goswami ji please continue, please continue to narrate more and more and more about the lila of the lord, my thirst for it increases the more I drink."

He said, “Unfortunately, those who are very poor hearted and attached to sense gratification and material enjoyment they do not seek out the endless and limitless pleasure of your transcendental narrations. Therefore my conclusion is these persons are the most unfortunate creatures on the earth, one who takes this rare human birth and does not take pleasure in hearing and chanting the glories of the lord are most unfortunate.” He said, “My dear Sukadev Goswami, one whose tongue is not glorifying the Lord, his tongue is like the croaking tongue of the frogs. One whose ears are not hearing the glories of the Lord, his ears are like the snake holes in the sand. One whose eyes do not relish the beauty of the form of the lord in the temple and the beauty of the Lord's devotee, his eyes have no more value than the tail of the peacock. And one whose head does not bow down to the Lord; his head is only is simply is only so much excess weight on his body.” When Sukadev Goswami heard this, he became very pleased. When the spiritual master sees that the disciple is sincere to perfect his life by hearing submissively spiritual subject matter, the spiritual master becomes most delighted and then he began to narrate one of the most beautiful stories of the Srimad Bhagvatam.

Story of Sudama Vipra

This story is famous thorough out all the worlds, we have all heard it, but unlike material subject matter, the more we hear these stories, the more each time we learn something fresh to help us in our spiritual lives. Sukadev Goswami, he explains that, “Once there was a very pious and religious brahmin his name was Sudama. He wasn't an ordinary brahmin, but he was a true devotee of the lord, his life. his soul was only dedicated to pleasing the lord in every way. Therefore, this Brahmin Sudama he was not at all concerned with his own material situation in life as a brahmin he simply accepted whatever came by the ways of destiny. He did not ever endeavour in any way, shape or form for the maintenance of his body or for the maintenance of his family. But he was always very peaceful, always very merciful to all living beings because of this, the great Sudama he was very poor. His house was practically un-livable, it was so simple, he only had one set of clothes, they were torn and even when they were washed they still appeared very dirty and he had so little food that he was barely living therefore he was very lean and thin and his bones were coming out of his body. And his poor wife was in the same condition.

So, one day his wife with great humility and respect, she approached her husband.” We should understand that his wife was not at all a demanding person, she factually wanted nothing for herself she was content simply to serve her husband and his mission. She expected nothing beyond his satisfaction. But it caused grief to her heart to see her husband in this condition, so with great humility she approached Sudama Vipra and she said, “My dear Sudama, you are very dear friends with Lord Sri Krishna, Dwarakadhish, the King of Dwaraka, he is living in Dwaraka in Sixteen Thousand One Hundred and Eight palaces and the Goddesses of fortune. Lakshmi Devi has expanded herself in each of these palaces and we all know that the lord is very kind to his devotees. The lord will do anything for his devotee. The lord will personally give his life to please his devotees. He will personally extend himself to any limit to satisfy any devotees. Sudama! at least, we should have the bare maintenance of the necessities of life, it would be absolutely no trouble for Krishna, he would be glad, he would be overjoyed to give you prosperity. It would make him happy; you think he likes to see us in this condition? Kindly go to Dwaraka and request your good friend Krishna, just for a little wealth.” She requested Sudama again and again and again but Sudama refused to go. He could not conceive of asking Krishna for anything except, “My Lord, how can I serve you? What can I give you?” This is the nature of the devotee and this is the nature of the lord. Amongst the pure devotees of the lord there is an endless competition. The Lord constantly wants to give to his devotees and the devotees never want to take anything from the Lord but want to give to him. And on this spiritual realm there is always a very tricky competition between the two.

Story of the Goswamis - Transcendental Competition between the Lord and His Devotees

There is a story, about Srila Sanatana Goswami, Sanatana Goswami was the elder brother of Rupa Goswami, so one-time Rupa Goswami wanted to cook a feast for Sanatana because it was his birthday. But they were simple Babajis living in Vrindavan, they had no money they would simply beg door to door for some chapatis every day. So how to cook a feast? but he was thinking my brother has gone out today I want to somehow or other cook a nice feast for him. So, at that time a young girl came to the house and said, “I heard it's Sanatana's birthday today, "Yes, Yes". Oh! I brought very nice vegetables and milk products and ghee ...everything… make a nice feast for him." He said, " Oh very nice!" So, he made a beautiful wonderful feat, he was an excellent wonderful cook. So Sanatana Goswami came back, “Aah! there was this wonderful feast, where did all this food come from?” “This beautiful little girl came, I don't know who she was, she had these pots on her head, and she gave me.” “Can you describe this little girl?” He described, Ahh... Sanatana said, “That is Srimati Radharani. How have you done this? You have engaged Radharani in my service. This is horrible, I am her servant. She is my lord and mistress of life and she is bringing food to me. How could you do this to me. The master should not serve the servant, the servant must be always be the servant of the master.” But you see, the lord is so obliged to the love of his devotees that the lord becomes subordinate to be the servant of his devotee. That is the natural inclination of God.

Story of Sudama Vipra - Continued

So, Sudama Vipra was not interested, but after the repeated requests of his very humble and virtuous wife, he finally decided ‘I must satisfy her. I will go to Dwarka just to have darshan with Sri Krishna, even if I don't ask him, even if I do not ask him for anything. Just to see him will be the greatest pleasure of my life.’ So, he decided to go but he said to his wife, “I must bring him some gift he is the greatest of all personalities, what do we have to give.” So she went to some neighbours' house and she took four handfuls of chipped rice, the cheapest most inexpensive of all foods and she put it in an old piece of cloth like a handkerchief and she wrapped it up, and she said, “Here, you give this to Krishna.” He said, “Alright”, and then immediately he went. And on his way, he was thinking ‘Ahh!! after so many years I will see the lord of my life Sri Krishna.’ So, he came to the outer limits of Dwaraka. Dwaraka was a very highly fortified fort, it was three military encampments that he had to pass through and it was very, very difficult to pass through these military camps but all of the soldiers had orders that any brahmins or holy men you must escort them through. Each of these military encampments had a very large, large gate separating from the next, Sudama Vipra passed through all without any difficulty and then when he came to the inner city of Dwarka he beheld with his eyes the most inconceivable sites there were Sixteen Thousand One Hundred and Eight palaces. Each one far excelling the beauty and the opulence of abode of Indra, the king of heaven. And he walked into the main palace. As he walked in Lord Sri Krishna was sitting on the bedstead with Rukmini and he saw Sudama coming from a small distance and immediately he got up from his bed and he greeted Sudama. This poor brahmin with torn clothes, dirty clothes, emaciated due to weakness and lack of nutrition to the mortal eyes he would appear the common beggar in the street, but he was not a beggar, he was the wealthiest of all men in creation. When Krishna saw him, he stood up and with tears of love in his eyes he approached Sudama and embraced him with great affection. As he embraced him his heart felt great ecstasy and tears flowed from his eyes. And then he took Sudama and then he put him on his own bedstead and then he brought him nice fresh water and then he brought him fresh fruits and then with his own hands he washed the lotus feet of Sudama Vipra and he took that water that had cascaded of the beautiful feet of Sudama and the Supreme Absolute Truth the personality of Godhead, Shyamsunder Vasudev he sprinkled that water on his head for his own purification and he sprinkled on his wife Rukmini’s head for her purification. The Supreme Lord, the yogis, they perform lifetimes of tapasya for the slightest glimpse of darshan of the lotus feet of the lord and the gyanis, for lifetimes they scrutinizingly study the Vedic literature and the scriptures of the world just for a glimpse of the feet of the lord. And the devotees they surrender their lives, body, minds, words and soul just to get a glimpse of the lotus feet of the lord. But the Lord, where is he finding his pleasure, in the feet of his devotees. This is the power of bhakti.

The impersonalist philosophers they say that, “If you perform religious discipline and the process of yoga, you can become God.” But the bhaktas are not interested in becoming God in fact it is an unpalatable thought to become God. One simply wants to love God and serve God and by the power of that love you can become better than God. How is that possible? A devotee never thinks he's better than God, but God comes to wash your feet.

Sudama Vipra, the Lord was sitting at his feet sprinkling the water on his head with great joy and then the Lord smeared his body with sandalwood powder, mixed with camphor in aguru and then he lit the most fragrant incense and offered it to Sudama Vipra and then he offered Aarti with the ghee lamp and the incense and the flower and then the handkerchief. While all this was going on the Supreme Goddess of Fortune Rukmini Devi in order to give comfort, to Sudama Vipra, with her own lotus hand fanned him with the Chamara.

After all these loving expressions of welcome to his devotee to the palace then lord Sri Krishna, he began to speak to Sudama, “How fortunate I am today that a holy foot of great brahmin like you have blessed my house. Sudama you know you are my dear friend. How are you? Please tell me.” When the ladies of the house, the maid servants of Sri Rukmini Dwaradhish saw this, they were stuck with wonder, ‘Who was this skinny little dirty man. Krishna embraced him.” They were talking amongst themselves, “Krishna embraced him! Did you see that? Krishna does not embrace anyone. The only person we have ever seen him embrace is Balrama and sometimes Rukmini. No one else does he embrace. Who is this person? What is his position? What is his glory?” Sudama Vipra and Lord Sri Krishna sat for many hours intimately discussing, Krishna said to Sudama that, “Do you remember, together we were classmates at the Gurukula of our Spiritual Master Sandipani Muni and do you remember all the wonderful things we learnt from him.” Krishna said, “I remember one thing, I am sure you will remember. One day the wife of our Guru Maharaj asked us to collect some firewood and we are collecting wood and we got lost and all of a sudden the wind began to blow very fast [Maharaj makes the sound of fast blowing wind] and dust came through the air and we could not see anything anywhere and the sky became very dark, the sunset and there was lightning and thunder ..[Maharaj makes sound of thunder] ..and then torrents of rain. No! it wasn't rain it was like the rainfall of devastation and the ground became muddy and flooded and we were young boys, we became very afraid. We did not know which direction to go, we go in one direction and then another direction and then next direction then another direction. We were thinking ‘Where is our home? Where is our guru?’ And we took each other by the hands and through the whole night we simply wandered through the darkness of the forest and the next morning Sandipani Muni, our Guru Maharaj, he learned that we were out all night, so he sent all the disciples out through the jungle to look for us and at last calling our names like our own father, he found us in the thick of the forest and when we saw our Guru Maharaj do you remember how happy we were, how we ran and bowed to his feet. How he embraced us and what he spoke to us? He said, “My dear children, just for me you have undergone so much trouble, this is the perfection of your life.”” The spiritual master gives transcendental knowledge and it is the duty of the disciple,

tad viddhi praṇipātena
paripraśnena sevayā
upadekṣyanti te jṣānaṁ
jṣāninas tattva-darśinaḥ

[Bg 4.34]

[Just try to learn the truth by approaching a spiritual master. Inquire from him submissively and render service unto him. The self-realized souls can impart knowledge unto you because they have seen the truth.]

To enquire, submissively from the guru, and render service. Humble menial service. And when the disciple is willing to accept tribulation, pain and suffering in order to please the spiritual master that is the only way that he can repay his debt to the guru. And when he sees that a disciple is willing to accept any inconvenience simply for the smallest pleasure of the guru, the spiritual master is so satisfied that he bestows all his blessings and mercy to such a lowly and unqualified disciple and then Sandipani Muni prayed, he said, “My dear Krishna and Sudama, I bless both of you today that all Vedic knowledge will be remembered by you for all the times and that whatever you desire in life will be fulfilled. And that you will always have peace, happiness in all circumstances.” And then Sudama Vipra hearing this narration, his heart chocked up with ecstasy, he began to speak to Lord Krishna, “My Lord! all my sacrifices, all my austerities, everything has become a success, only because you are allowing me to be your friend, my lord there is no need for you to have a spiritual master. You are the Absolute Truth, you are the Jagad Guru, the master of all but just to show all living beings that everyone must have a spiritual master to understand God, you accept a spiritual master yourself.” In Bhagvad Gita Krishna says,

yad yad ācarati śreṣṭhas
tat tad evetaro janaḥ
sa yat pramāṇaṁ kurute
lokas tad anuvartate

[Bg 3.21]

[Whatever action a great man performs, common men follow. And whatever standards he sets by exemplary acts, all the world pursues.]

what great men do common men follow in his footsteps whatever example the great men do all the world pursues, “Therefore my lord although you are the knower of past, present and future, you are in the heart of every living being as the parmatama, you are vedaiś ca sarvair aham eva vedyo
vedānta-kṛd veda-vid eva cāham [g]. Although you are the author of the vedas, the knower of all the vedas still you accepted a guru to learn the vedas, it is inconceivable. Why? Only to show all humanity that however learned you are, however, brilliant you may think you may be, however pious, religious your family may be. Without accepting a bona fide spiritual master as one's guide in life, no one can understand the vedas, no one can understand the truth of, what is god. therefore, my dear lord how sweet and beautiful is this pastime that you became the humble menial servant of your guru and how lucky I am that I got to be a part of that leela.” Krishna said to Sudama that, “My dear Sudama, it is true that I am the paramatma within every one's heart and I am the witness of everyone's act, every word and every thought and therefore in order to guide society properly I am very much expecting great brahmins like you to establish the proper system of varna ashram for all humanity to follow.”

Varnashrama System

Varnaashram means, brahmin, ksatriya, vaishyas and shudras, brahmcharis, grihastas, vanaprasthas and sannyasis. Krishna told Sudama that cātur-varṇyaṁ mayā sṛṣṭaṁ guṇa-karma-vibhāgaśaḥ [Bg 4.13] that according to one's natural qualities and one's actions one is categorized in this varnaashrama system. Nowhere does Krishna say anything about birth. He says guna and karma, he does not mention janma. and this is the great disgrace and pollution of society especially in India. That person with no qualification simply because they are born in a particular family, they expect the respect deserved by that caste. The caste system is the total pollution and corruption of the original pure science given by God himself. In Bhagvad Gita Krishna describes what are the qualities of a brahmin, peacefulness, self-control, mercifulness, he is learned in the scriptures. he is not possessive, his whole life is simply dedicated as a sacrifice for the upliftment of humanity, if one does not have these qualifications, he is not a brahmin. It has nothing to do with how you are born. If you are born in an untouchable family but if you have these qualities, you should be respected as a brahmin. And if you were born in a brahmin family but if you have the qualities of an untouchable you should be understood to be untouchable, shudra. It is a question of one's qulalities. So, Krishna was appreciating for being a pure hearted and genuine brahmin.

And then Lord Krishna looked at Sudama and he said, “I think that you have brought me some gift, I think your wife and brought ...prepared some nice food to offer to me.” And Sudama was feeling very much ashamed, He was looking around that this beautiful palace and he just tasted the finest foods that Krishna offered him and he looked at Rukmini the Goddess of fortune herself and he is thinking ‘how am I going to give Krishna this paltry offering of chipped rice.’ He said “No, Krishna I have not brought anything”, Krishna said, “Sudama, Do you think that I taste anything material? I only taste the love of my devotee - patraṁ puṣpaṁ phalaṁ toyaṁ yo me bhaktyā prayacchati [g ]. If one even offers me a leaf a little water a little flower with devotion it brings me the greatest pleasure and happiness. Sudama you are my devotee! even if you have only brought me a little chipped rice because of your love it will be the most palatable sumptuous feast for my heart but know if a wealthy man offers me the most valuable things the most valuable gifts the most succulent preparations if they are offered with pride, if they are not offered with humility and devotion, I do not care to accept those things they mean nothing to me. Krishna said to Sudama. Do you think I am in need of anything? I am the supreme proprietor of everything that exists. But even the smallest offering by the poorest simplest devotee if it is offered with love it means more to me than my whole abode of Vaikuntha.” And then Krishna told Sudama that, “You have voluntarily accepted a life of poverty because you are not at all interested in the things of this material world. This is the proof of your knowledge, many people boast that they are the knowers of the truth but the real test of one who really knows, what is the truth that it is detached from material enjoyment. Therefore, Sudama you have absolutely no affinity to possess anything of this world because you know everything belongs to me. This is your glory. You are not attached to sense gratification, but you are only attached to serving God, therefore you are the wealthiest man in all of creation.” One who possesses this love is the wealthiest man in all of creation. But even if one is multi- multi- multi billionaire without this love, his heart is like a ghetto, it is poverty, it is worthless. Real wealth is in internal condition of consciousness because this external show cannot satisfy anyone. But then Krishna explained to Sudama, “But there are some very wealthy people but in their hearts they are always thinking how everything is my property and they use everything in my glorification therefore those wealthy people, even great kings living in palatial buildings they satisfy me equally.”

He explains that, “I am setting this example and you are setting the other example.” Krishna said, “Although I a God, I am trying to set the proper example for humanity. Here I have Sixteen Thousand One Hundred and Eight palaces, huh... I have great wealth. But every day I am giving charity, I am doing puja. Every day I am doing the religious acts to show that even the wealthiest man in creation, their wealth is not a disqualification for spiritual life so long as they are not attached to greed. So long as they are seeing God's energy everywhere and dedicating everything they do and everything they earn in God's glorification. And you Sudama, are setting the example of how a brahmin can live with absolutely nothing and please the Lord with the ultimate wealth of your love. So Sudama please give me the chipped rice.” And then Krishna, he knows everything! he doesn't have to look very hard. He saw hanging from Sudama's shoulder was this little bag of chipped rice and Krishna snatched it from him and he opened it and when he saw this chipped rice he said..., “Aaah!” he said, “Simply by my taking one morsel of this chipped rice the whole universe will be satisfied, not only you Sudama.” Bhagvatam describes that God is like the root of the tree when Krishna is nourished the whole creation is nourished. If you water the root of the tree, every leaf, every branch, every twig, everything is satisfied. So similarly, when we satisfy Krishna the root of all that exists, Aham sarvasva … mattahayo… [Bg 10.8]. Then everyone becomes nourished by that loving service to the Lord. Then Krishna with great pleasure, he ate one morsel of that rice then he went to ate the next then Rukmini the Goddess of fortune stopped him with her hand and she said, “My dear Lord, anyone who satisfies you even with one morsel of bhoga that is offered with love, that person is destined to have prosperity both in this world and in the next.” Krishna is Lakshminath, he is husband of goddess of fortune. If one offers with love and devotion within one's home, food to Narayana. Then Narayan himself guarantees that he will enter into your home to accept that food and there is nowhere Narayan goes that Lakshmi does not also come. And wherever there is Lakshmi then there is all good fortune. So, there is no need to worry about our material or spiritual condition of life, if we know we are satisfying the lord by humbly offering in our devotion. And Lakshmi and Narayana will live within our home. They will live within our life and there will be no shortage.

So after this most sweet and wonderful leela..aah..Sudama vipra bid lord Sri Krishna goodbye and then he began to walk home and he was filled with ecstatic love, he was simply remembering ‘How Krishna embraced me, how he washed my feet, how he put me on his own bed, how Rukmini fanned me, how he spoke so intimately with me, so merciful is the Lord, how the Lord loves his devotees, how the Lord loves his devotees, how the lord will do anything and everything for his devotee who surrenders to his lotus feet. He is the most wonderful merciful and suitable master to take shelter of.’ And he was just enraptured in divine ecstasy thinking of the mercy of the Lord and as he came close to his home, he began to think ‘how will I explained to my wife I did not ask Krishna for anything? But certainly, she will understand.’ And then when he came to the property of his residence, lo and behold what he saw he could not believe. There was the most beautiful palace made out of the finest marble bedecked with jewels and gold towering into the sky and between the palaces there were beautiful parks with lakes, with flocks of auspicious birds singing delightful songs and peacocks dancing and swans swimming and lotus flowers blooming everywhere and there were the most beautiful, beautiful human beings strolling through these parks singing beautiful songs. He was thinking ‘Where am I?’

And all of a sudden, some people spotted him. Beautiful, beautiful human beings they looked like demigods they would be decked with the finest clothes and ornaments and musical bands following behind him they all ran to him with smiles upon their faces to greet him and they put garlands around him and told "Welcome Home". And then he saw his beloved wife and she approached him, she approached her husband, her eyes were swollen with tears she was so choked up she could not say a word all she could do was bow her head at his feet and in her heart she was embracing him.

And Sudama looked at his wife she had beautiful clothes and beautiful jewellery she looked like the wife of a demigod and his eyes filled with tears and he was choked up he was thinking ‘How is this possible, this can only happen by the mercy of Krishna my beloved friend. I did not even ask him for anything but simply because he was satisfied with the little chipped rice look at what he has given me in return.’ He could not say a word to his wife, both being speechless not at the great fortune and opulence that they had obtained this is not what brought them joy in ecstasy but the sweetness of the love of Krishna upon them, that overwhelmed their hearts and in complete silence with their hearts over flooding with devotion ecstasy they both walked into the house. And then his good wife brought him to his quarters and when he saw his quarters, he was struck with wonder even Indra had never seen such quarters as this.

Everything was structured out of translucent, transparent marble and the couches and the beds were made out of the finest ivory decorated with precious emeralds and stones and jewels, diamonds and gold and there were beautiful canopies and there were strings of the finest pearls decorating in all directions.

And everything he saw in his house he did not see it that ‘How can I enjoy it.’ But he simply saw Krishna in everything. He saw Krishna's mercy in everything. He saw all of his wealth, all of his fortune as the divine blessing of God and therefore whatever he saw simply increased his love for Krishna. He and his wife lived in that setting for many years, but they never considered themselves the proprietors or the enjoyers but everything they had they utilized for the pleasure of God Krishna says, in Bhagavad Gita,

yat karoṣi yad aśnāsi
yaj juhoṣi dadāsi yat
yat tapasyasi kaunteya
tat kuruṣva mad-arpaṇam

[Bg 9.27]

[Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer or give away, and whatever austerities you perform – do that, O son of Kuntī, as an offering to Me.

...all that you do, all that you eat, all that you offer and give away should be done as an offering of love to me.

bhoktāraṁ yajṣa-tapasāṁ
sarva-loka-maheśvaram
suhṛdaṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ
jṣātvā māṁ śāntim ṛcchati

[Bg 5.29]

[A person in full consciousness of Me, knowing Me to be the ultimate beneficiary of all sacrifices and austerities, the Supreme Lord of all planets and demigods, and the benefactor and well-wisher of all living entities, attains peace from the pangs of material miseries. ]

Conclusion - Become Humble to get the Mercy of the Lord

Lord Sri Krishna describes the Gita that “One who sees me the proprietor of everything that exists and who uses everything for my enjoyment and trust that I am the loving friend of my devotee such a person attains the ultimate peace of life.” So you see this story of Sudama Vipra teaches us whether we are a rich man or a poor man doesn't matter it's not that you have to become poor to get the mercy of God and it's not that you have to become rich to get the mercy of God you simply have to become humble to get the mercy of God. What does it mean to be humble that, “I am nothing I am simply dependent on the mercy of the lord and his devotees and whatever I have, let me with remembrance of god with love for god let me use everything for his pleasure I have a family this is God's family it's not my family let me raise this family in a wonderful way for the pleasure of the Lord of my life. Let me raise them the way he wants them to be raised so that they never have to suffer birth and death again so that they can live a happy life in this world a prosperous life in this world and they can attain real peace and return to Vaikuntha.” This is human civilization so Sukadev Goswami ends this narration of Sudama Vipra by offering the benediction that “Anyone with a sincere heart and with faith hears this most holy lila, that person will understand how Krishna loves his devotee and anyone who understands how Krishna loves his devotee who want nothing else in life as a goal except to be Krishna's devotee and to have that love and will be willing to do anything or give anything to have that love that relationship, that friendship with Krishna. And he offers the benediction that, “Anyone who hears with faith all prosperity will be back upon him because he will have the supreme blessings of the Goddess of Fortune and he will very soon return to the spiritual world Vaikuntha.” Indeed, Sukadeva Goswami described that, “Sudama Vipra lived on those beautiful conditions in loving memory of Krishna and a few years later he and his wife left this mortal world for the spiritual world Vaikuntha where they attained the eternal destination of the soul.”

And exactly what was available to Sudama Vipra is actually available to everyone today if we simply make Krishna at the centre of our life, if we simply associate with holy people who remind us that Krishna is the goal of life. The association of sadhus is the most valuable precious attainment one can achieve in life. Because through that association we can understand the love of Krishna for his devotees and we can understand the nature of the love within our own heart for Krishna.

And the greatest welfare work, the greatest charity the greatest expression of friendship and love is to share Krishna with another. Therefore we are very pleased with the host of our evening tonight whose house we are now so graciously enjoying Krishna Katha and we should know that he has properly understood what is the greatest charity, what is the greatest friendship and greatest love.

To invite friends, visitors, well-wishers to come into one's home, to chant the glories of the Lord, to hear the glories of the Lord, to taste the prasada of the Lord, to associate with one another on this purest level of social interaction, this is the greatest charity this is the greatest friendship this is the greatest expression of love and this is really the fulfilment of the duty of the Grihasta ashram that whatever we have from time to time let us share it with everyone for the glorification of God. That is the success of our life that is the purification of our house the purification of our family, the purification of our existence. So we are very much grateful this evening for our kind host for giving us this opportunity of coming into his and her most beautiful house and decorating it with all of these most magnanimous effulgent guests who have all come very anxiously to perfect their lives by hearing the glories of the Lord.

Spiritual life is very simple, simply by always being anxious to find Krishna within our heart through the association of devotees and share Krishna with others we can attain the supreme perfection of life and the common denominator according to all the great scriptures by which we can exchange the pure presence of Krishna is the congregational chanting of his holy name.

Sankirtana means when we come together to glorify the Lord by discussing his pastimes, by discussing his philosophy and especially by chanting his name's then we create the atmosphere of Vaikunta wherever we are. Tonight, this house has become Vaikunta and this is confirmed by all the Shastra yashaham... What is that verse? tatra tisthami narada yatra gayanti mad-bhaktah [ŚB 4.30.35 purport]. Krishna tells Narada Muni that, “You will not find me in the caves where the Yogi's are meditating you will not find me in the universities where the gyanis are studying their Scriptures. You will not find me where the great kings are offering sacrifices and Yagya but wherever an assembly of my devotees come together to chat my holy names I will always be in that place. I choose to be there better than anywhere else where there is high, high philosophy being spoken I am not so attracted but where the simple hearts of my devotees are simply glorifying my name's I cannot resist from running to that place.” And this is a fact.

Krishna is Vaikunthanath he is the Lord of Vaikuntha. He is Vrindavananath he never takes one step out of Vrindavan. The fact is wherever the devotees gathered together to hear the glories of the Lord and to chant his holy names that place is Vaikunta, that place is Goloka Vrindavan. we may not have the vision to perceive it but if we just stay in the holy dham long enough we will develop that vision. So tonight, my friends you are in Vaikuntha, you are in Vrindavan. In truth, in fact, it is the word of God and Krishna is here.

So, let us take these opportunities as much as possible, to associate on this level it is really the opening the doors to the highest perfection of life. [Break and Lecture Ends]

© Radhanath Swami Lecture Transcripts.
